title: T4 Ligation
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"99uo6","text":"Reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ofdh","text":"T4 ligase (Thermofisher, 15224041)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"57n35","text":"5x T4 ligation Buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfmpd","text":"Competent E. coli cells (NEB, C3040H)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3i721","text":"LB plates with necessary antibiotic","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1. For initial reaction, Mix: X and Y should be calculated for 3 Insert:1 vector molar ratio Example of Molar Ratio calculation for 3000bp vector with 500bp PCR product insert: A B C D E 1 Component Length of DNA (bp) Molar ratio ng of DNA Volume of 50ng/ul solution 2 Vector 3000 1 50 1 ul 3 PCR Fragment 500 3 25 0.5 ul 4 H20 8.5 ul 2. Incubate at room temperature for 00:15:00 . 3. Transform Product into E. coli a) Add 2 µL of product to 50 µL of TOP10 cells and pipette up and down slowly to mix. b) Incubate for 00:20:00 on ice. c) Heat shock bacteria in 42 °C waterbath for 00:01:00 . d) Incubate on Ice for 00:03:00 . e) Add 100 µL of SOC media and shake in warm room for 01:00:00 . f) Plate bacteria onto LB-Antibiotic Plate and spread cells with plate spreader to get individual colonies. g) Incubate overnight in 37 °C warm room. h) Pick colonies for miniprep growth and sequencing.
T4 DNA Ligase catalyzes the joining of two cohesive- or blunt-ended strands of DNA between the 5´-phosphate and the 3´-hydroxyl groups of adjacent nucleotides.
creator: Cecilia Escudero
